idle.eeg_auto_stop_hint: If a seizure starts, any active EEG activity is stopped automatically and a seizure reference is added to the EEG log.

recording.video_hint: Start only if it is safe and helpful to capture the seizure.
recording.video_error_unavailable: Camera permission or video recording is not available on this device.

alert.dont_leave: Do not leave the patient unattended

tagging.post_ictal_paralysis_help: Record Todd's paralysis with exact affected body parts.

export.title: Export & Reports
export.backup_json_desc: Full encrypted data snapshot for backup and device migration. AuraTrack encrypts the file before it is saved.
export.csv_desc: Events, medications, and dose logs in comma-separated format. Opens in Excel / Sheets.
export.pdf_desc: Printable event log with notes and symptom paths. Opens an in-app preview so you can print or save as PDF.
export.privacy_notice_body: Encrypted backups are protected with your passphrase before they are saved. CSV, PDF, and video exports are still regular files, so treat them like medical documents.
export.neuro_report_desc: Purpose-built clinical PDF — patient info, phase timings, symptom breakdown, and full event log. Formatted for presenting to a neurologist or specialist.
export.neuro_report_warning: ⚠ Add patient name in Settings → Identity for a complete report.
export.ios_pdf_hint: On iPhone/iPad, tap Print to save this as a PDF.
export.diary_desc: One-page monthly seizure diary with colour-coded event days. Opens an in-app preview for printing or PDF save.

docs.ictal_symptom_log: Ictal Symptom Log — All Period Events
docs.no_symptoms_period: No symptoms recorded in this period.
docs.no_clinical_flags: No clinical flags detected in this reporting period.
docs.disclaimer_intro: This report was generated by AuraTrack v{{version}}, a personal seizure-logging PWA. All data was entered by the caretaker or patient.
docs.disclaimer_medical: This document is not a substitute for professional medical assessment. Times and durations are based on manual input.
docs.disclaimer_edited: Phase durations marked as edited may differ from real-time capture.
docs.diary_footer: Generated by AuraTrack · {{date}} · All data recorded by caretaker or patient. Not a substitute for professional medical assessment.
docs.expected_doses: (expected ~{{count}} for this regimen over selected period)
docs.no_dose_logs: No dose logs recorded in this period.
docs.no_data_available: No data available for this period
docs.flag_detail_recent_vs_prior: {{recent}} events in the most recent 15 days vs {{prior}} in the prior 15 days.
docs.flag_detail_auto_terminated: Timer stopped automatically at 12 minutes due to patient unresponsiveness; may indicate a prolonged recovery state.
docs.flag_detail_edited_records: Phase durations or details were modified after initial recording and may differ from real-time capture.
docs.no_wellbeing_entries: No wellbeing entries recorded in this period.

export.eeg_desc: Export the most recent EEG session as a table-first clinical PDF.

event_detail.title: Event Detail
event_detail.danger_prolonged_desc: This event lasted more than 5 minutes. Seek medical review if not already done.
event_detail.danger_cluster_desc: {{count}} seizures occurred within 8 minutes. Consult a neurologist immediately.
event_detail.video_saved_hint: The file is saved in device storage and referenced here for the neurologist record.

recording.title: Recording a Seizure
steps: Tap the large START button when a seizure begins.
In Caretaker mode, tap the phase buttons (End Aura, End Seizure) as each phase transitions. In Self mode the interface is simplified to a single stop button.
Tap + Event Note to stamp a timestamped note mid-recording — use the shortcut buttons for common events like Fell, Rescue Med given, Not Responding, etc.
Tap STOP when the seizure ends. If the timer is still running at 12 minutes, the recording stops automatically and the event is saved.

tagging.title: Tagging Events
steps: After stopping, a tagging wizard opens automatically.
Select the seizure type, affected body regions, and any symptoms observed.
Add free-text notes, then tap SAVE. You can edit any event later from History.

manual.title: Manual Entry
steps: Tap + Log Past Seizure on the home screen to enter a seizure you did not record in real time.
Set the date, time, and duration, then complete the tagging wizard as usual.

medications.title: Medication Tracking
steps: Add your medications in Settings → Medications.
The home screen shows today's scheduled doses — tap each medication button to toggle it, then tap Save to log the doses.
Enable dose reminders per medication in Settings → Medications.

history.title: History & Export
steps: Open HISTORY to browse, search, and filter all recorded events.
Tap any event to view full detail or edit it.
Use EXPORT (from History) to generate a PDF neurologist report, CSV, or encrypted backup.

settings.title: Settings & Accessibility
steps: Switch between Self and Caretaker mode in Settings to adjust the interface.
Change theme, font size, and accent colour to suit your preferences.
Add your neurologist's name and emergency contacts for quick access during a seizure.

about.title: About
about.about_text: AuraTrack is a privacy-first seizure tracking app for people with epilepsy and their caregivers. Record seizures in real time, annotate events with clinical detail, track medications, and generate structured reports for your neurologist — all stored locally on your device.
about.privacy_text: AuraTrack is designed to keep seizure and medication records local-first. By default, your data stays in your browser storage on this device and is only shared when you deliberately export, print, or sync it.

local.title: Stored locally
local.body: AuraTrack stores event logs, medications, dose logs, EEG diary entries, and report settings in IndexedDB on your device. The app does not require an account to create or keep these records.

sharing.title: When data is shared
sharing.body: Your health data leaves AuraTrack only when you choose to export a backup or report, print a PDF, save a video recording, or send data to another device during sync.

sync.title: Sync behavior
sync.body: AuraTrack's device-to-device sync uses browser-encrypted transport. Depending on the sync mode, connection or signaling metadata may still pass through the signaling service or your local network.

exports.title: Export security
exports.body: Encrypted backups are protected by the passphrase you choose. Exported CSV, PDF, and video files remain regular files on the destination device.

about.disclaimer_text: AuraTrack is a personal logging tool and is not a medical device. It is not intended to diagnose, treat, or replace professional medical advice. Always consult a qualified healthcare provider regarding your condition and treatment.
about.opensource_text: AuraTrack is open source. Report bugs or suggest features on GitHub Issues.

identity.mode_desc_caretaker: Recording for another person. Full phase timing controls visible during recording.
identity.mode_desc_self: Recording your own events. Simplified one-button interface.

recording.haptic_help: Vibration on button presses (device must support it)
recording.eeg_diary_help: Turn this on only when long-term EEG monitoring is needed. The EEG diary controls stay hidden on the idle screen unless enabled.
recording.quick_labels_help: Customise the shortcut buttons shown during recording. Leave blank to hide a button.
recording.wellbeing_help: Optional mood and context logging for pattern spotting.
recording.wellbeing_reminder_help: Optional reminder. It is skipped after a wellbeing entry is saved for the day.
recording.wellbeing_factors_help: Choose the context fields shown in the wellbeing sheet. Existing diary entries keep their saved labels.

medications.section_desc: Current medication regimen. Used in reports and dose-logging.
medications.dose_reminders_help: Push notification at each scheduled time
medications.start_date_help: The earliest date shown in medication history. Leave blank to auto-detect from records.
medications.reminder_granted_help: Reminders will fire while app is open or suspended.
medications.reminder_denied_help: Blocked in browser settings — reset site permissions to re-enable.
medications.reminder_off_help: Enable to receive dose reminder notifications.
medications.ios_reminder_note: On iOS, the app must be installed to your home screen for notifications to work. Notifications require the app to be running or suspended — they cannot be delivered when the app is fully closed.

reports.section_desc: Information used to generate clinical neurologist reports.
reports.include_dob_help: Whether DOB appears in printed reports
reports.notes_placeholder: Medications, known triggers, clinician instructions, or anything else to include at the end of reports...

data.persistence_help_checking: AuraTrack is checking whether the browser will protect this PWA storage from eviction.
data.persistence_help_granted: Browser-managed storage has been marked persistent. Data should not be evicted except by explicit user action.
data.persistence_help_denied: The browser did not grant persistent storage. Backups remain the main protection against data loss.
data.persistence_help_unsupported: This browser does not expose persistent storage controls. Backups remain the main protection against data loss.
data.persistence_help_error: AuraTrack could not confirm persistent storage protection on this device. Backups remain the main protection against data loss.
data.last_backup_help: Updated when an encrypted backup file is saved successfully.
data.backup_reminders_help: Reminders appear when no successful backup has been created within the chosen interval.
data.security_current: AuraTrack backups are exported as encrypted files protected by your chosen passphrase.
data.security_exports: CSV, PDF, and video exports are not encrypted by AuraTrack. Anyone who can open those files can read their contents.
data.security_sync: Device-to-device sync uses browser-encrypted transport, but sync setup can still expose connection metadata depending on whether you use PeerJS signaling or local-network QR exchange.
data.security_recommendation: Keep your backup passphrase somewhere safe. AuraTrack cannot recover forgotten backup passphrases.
data.clear_confirm: This permanently deletes recorded events, medications, and medication dose logs. Cannot be undone.
data.reset_confirm: Reset all preferences to factory defaults?
data.delete_failed: Failed to delete data. Please try again.
data.import_failed: Import failed — invalid or corrupted file.
data.persistence_help_not_requested: This browser may show a permission prompt, so AuraTrack waits for you to request storage protection manually.
data.persistence_detail_flow_prompt: User-initiated request. A browser permission prompt may appear when you tap the button.
data.persistence_detail_flow_auto: Automatic browser decision. The browser may silently grant or deny without showing a permission popup.
data.persistence_detail_browser_webkit: WebKit browsers on iPhone/iPad and Safari-class environments usually decide this silently. Installing the app can help storage quotas, but backups are still essential.
data.persistence_detail_browser_chromium: Chromium browsers such as Chrome and Edge usually decide this from browser heuristics, so tapping again may recheck without showing a prompt.
data.persistence_detail_browser_gecko: Firefox-class browsers may show a permission prompt when requested from this screen.
data.persistence_detail_browser_generic: Browser behavior varies by engine and device.
data.persistence_detail_reason: Exact denial reasons are not exposed by the web API, so AuraTrack can only show the browser result, not why it decided that way.

about_section.reinstall_sub: Tap if you dismissed the install banner by mistake

modal.passphrase_short_hint: Use at least 8 characters. A few memorable words works well.
modal.passphrase_ok_hint: Keep this passphrase somewhere safe. AuraTrack cannot recover it later.
modal.passphrase_short_error: Passphrase must be at least 8 characters.
modal.no_data: No health data available to back up.
modal.export_failed: Encrypted backup could not be saved.
modal.passphrase_required: Enter the backup passphrase to continue.
modal.not_encrypted: Selected file is not an encrypted backup.
modal.import_bad_passphrase: Incorrect passphrase or damaged backup file.
modal.export_body: AuraTrack will encrypt the backup before saving it. The file name can be changed in the save dialog.
modal.export_warning: If you forget this passphrase, the backup cannot be recovered.
modal.import_body: AuraTrack will decrypt this backup in memory, import it, and will not write a plaintext copy to device storage.

reminder.title: Create a fresh backup
reminder.body: It has been more than {{days}} days since the last successful backup. A new encrypted backup helps recover data after browser resets, site-data deletion, or device migration.

delete_modal.title: DELETE RECORD?
delete_modal.body: This medical entry will be permanently removed. This action cannot be undone.

manual_entry.title: Log Past Seizure
manual_entry.intro: Enter the date, time, and duration of the seizure. You can then add the seizure type, symptoms, triggers, and notes.
manual_entry.error_duration: Total duration must be greater than 0.

event_card.notes_placeholder: Clinical observations, after-seizure notes, medication given...
event_card.cluster_title: Cluster seizures / Status Epilepticus risk - {{count}} events within 8 minutes

summary.notes_placeholder: Clinical observations, after-seizure notes, medication given...

eeg.title: EEG Diary
eeg.description: EEG sessions stay separate from seizure history. Seizures recorded during EEG appear here as linked reference rows.
eeg.no_activities: No EEG activities logged for this session.
eeg.linked_description: This entry is linked to the seizure record and keeps its timing from that event.

wellbeing.idle_hint: Mood and context are optional, but help with pattern spotting.
wellbeing.chart_help: This view helps spot patterns. It does not prove that any factor caused a seizure.
wellbeing.notification_body: Add mood and context while it is still fresh.

moods.content_help: Okay or satisfied, comfortable with things as they are.
moods.sad_low_help: Down, unhappy, withdrawn, or less interested than usual.
moods.anxious_worried_help: Nervous, fearful, or expecting something bad.
moods.stressed_overloaded_help: Under pressure, too much happening, hard to cope.
moods.restless_agitated_help: Unable to settle, pacing, keyed up, or visibly activated.
moods.tired_sleepy_help: Low energy, drowsy, or wanting to sleep.
moods.confused_help: Disoriented, unclear, or having trouble following what is happening.
moods.emotionally_flat_help: Little visible emotion or reduced response.
moods.very_happy_euphoric_help: Unusually high, excited, or intensely positive mood.

factors.sleep_hours_help: Approximate hours slept since the last check-in.
factors.sleeplessness_help: Long awake periods, very delayed sleep, or very broken sleep.
factors.missed_bowel_movement_help: No bowel movement when one was expected for the person.
factors.stress_help: Pressure, overload, conflict, or demanding events.
factors.illness_fever_help: Signs of illness, infection, fever, or feeling unwell.
factors.missed_medication_help: Any scheduled medicine was missed or significantly delayed.
factors.hormonal_menstruation_help: Menstruation, hormonal changes, or suspected cycle-related symptoms.
factors.unusual_activity_help: A day that was more active, different, or stimulating than usual.
factors.tearful_crying_help: Crying or visibly tearful, whether or not the person could describe why.

sync.import_summary_with_wellbeing: Added {{events}} event(s), {{medications}} medication(s), {{logs}} dose log(s), {{eegSessions}} EEG session(s), {{eegActivities}} EEG activity record(s), and {{wellbeingEntries}} wellbeing entries.
sync.role_locked: Role locked because this page was opened from a sync QR code.
sync.already_up_to_date: Both devices were already up to date - no new records added.
sync.already_up_to_date_short: Already up to date - no new records.
sync.scan_qr_prompt: Point camera at the QR code
sync.scan_sender_prompt: Point camera at the sender device QR code
sync.camera_denied: Camera access denied. Allow camera permission and try again.
sync.easy_sender_intro: This device will send its AuraTrack data to another device over the internet. Start here, then let the receiving device scan the QR code.
sync.easy_receiver_intro: This device will receive AuraTrack data from another device over the internet. On the sender device, open Easy Sync and scan its QR code with this device.
sync.easy_security: Data travels peer-to-peer over a browser-encrypted connection. A PIN confirms both devices are yours.
sync.easy_metadata: PeerJS is used for connection signaling only; sync still shares metadata such as connection IDs.
sync.easy_step_1: 1. Open Easy Sync on the sender device.
sync.easy_step_2: 2. Scan the sender QR with this device.
sync.easy_step_3: 3. Compare the PINs and press Start Sync on this receiving device.
sync.verify_pin_both: Security PIN - verify on both screens
sync.waiting_receiver_connect: Waiting for the receiver device to connect... (90s)
sync.pin_match_question: Does this PIN match the sender device?
sync.verify_pin_receiver: Verify this PIN on the receiver device
sync.waiting_receiver_confirm: Waiting for the receiver device to confirm the PIN...
sync.private_sender_intro: This device will send its AuraTrack data directly over the same WiFi network. No relay server is used after setup.
sync.private_receiver_intro: This device will receive AuraTrack data directly over the same WiFi network. Start on the sender device, then scan its connection QR from this device.
sync.private_security: Signaling travels via QR codes only - no relay server. Your data and metadata stay on your local network.
sync.private_scans: Requires two QR scans: receiver scans sender offer QR, then sender scans receiver answer QR.
sync.private_step_1: 1. Open Private Sync on the sender device.
sync.private_step_2: 2. Scan the sender connection QR with this device.
sync.private_step_3: 3. Show the answer QR back to the sender device.
sync.step1_receiver_scans: Step 1: Receiver scans this QR code
sync.after_receiver_scan: After the receiver scans this, it will show an answer QR
sync.show_to_sender: Show this to the sender device to scan
sync.waiting_sender_scan: Waiting for the sender device to scan this QR code...
sync.manual_sender_intro: Create an encrypted AuraTrack backup on this device and send that file to the receiving device by AirDrop, Files, email, or cloud storage.
sync.manual_receiver_intro: Import an encrypted AuraTrack backup from another device. Safe to repeat - duplicates are automatically skipped.
sync.invalid_qr: That QR code is not a valid AuraTrack sync code.
sync.title: Sync to Another Device
